translations.verbatim_quote: A true yogī observes Me in all beings and also sees every being in Me. Indeed, the self-realized person sees Me, the same Supreme Lord, everywhere.
A true yogi observes Me in all beings and also sees every being in Me. Indeed, the self-realized person sees Me, the same Supreme Lord, everywhere.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 2030 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: A Kṛṣṇa conscious yogī is the perfect seer because he sees Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme, situated in everyone’s heart as Supersoul (Paramātmā). Īśvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ hṛd-deśe ’rjuna tiṣṭhati. The Lord in His Paramātmā feature is situated within both the heart of the dog and that of a brāhmaṇa.

Inequalities pertain only to Prakrti or the bodies they are embodied in. One whose mind is fixed in Y

positions.rendering: A true yogī observes Me in all beings and also sees every being in Me
vision of sameness everywhere — knowledge of the identity of the Self and Brahman everywhere, in all conditioned states
all selves separated from Prakṛti are by Themselves only of the nature of knowledge; inequalities pertain only to Prakṛti / bodies

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Sama-darśanaḥ is read by Shankara as ontological identity (one Brahman in all); by Ramanuja as functional similarity (all selves identical-in-nature, inequalities residing in Prakṛti); by Prabhupada as theistic perception (Lord in all beings). The traditions agree the verse names equal vision; they differ on whether the ground is non-duality, qualified non-duality, or theistic immanence.

literal_meaning: The yogi whose self is yoked sees the Self situated in all beings, and all beings in the Self; everywhere, of equal vision.
traditional_meaning_consensus: 6.29 names the doctrine of sama-darśana (equal vision) as the fruit of yoked-self perception. The yogi yoga-yukta-ātmā sees the ātman sarva-bhūta-stham — present in all beings — and sarva-bhūtāni ātmani — all beings present in the Self. Shankara: one whose mind is Self-absorbed through Yoga; vision of sameness everywhere; knowledge of the identity of Self and Brahman in all conditioned states (heaven, hell, animal, human). Ramanuja: separated from Prakṛti, all selves are by Themselves only of the nature of knowledge; inequalities pertain only to Prakṛti / bodies. Per chapter-6 thesis: STRONG analog at engineering scope — the senior who sees themselves in every junior they once were. Reflexive engineering empathy across roles. The metaphysical claim that all selves share substantial identity exceeds operational reach. STRETCHED at full scope; STRONG at narrowed empathy scope.
